name: Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
note: Ibn Saud united the Arabian Peninsula through conquest and treaty (1902–1932). Oil discovered in commercial quantities (1938) transformed the kingdom. The 1973 Oil Embargo made Saudi Arabia a global power. The Grand Mosque seizure (1979) by Juhayman al-Otaibi shocked the kingdom; the regime's response was to give more power to conservative ulema — the origin of Saudi export of Wahhabism worldwide. The kingdom remains an absolute monarchy under the Al Saud family, with Vision 2030 attempting modernisation under Crown Prince MBS.

figures.name: Ibn Saud
figures.significance: United Arabia through 30 years of warfare and diplomacy; signed the oil concession with Standard Oil (1933); the Aramco deal that created modern Saudi Arabia

figures.name: Faisal
figures.significance: Modernised Saudi Arabia; led the 1973 Oil Embargo against the West for supporting Israel; gave women rights to education; assassinated by his nephew

figures.name: MBS
figures.significance: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Salman; Vision 2030 modernisation; allowed women to drive; but also the killing of Khashoggi (2018) and Yemen War — reformer and autocrat simultaneously
